report_name: Lapsus$ Group Claims Breach of Spanish Insurer MAPFRE, States Data Was Stolen for a 'Private Party'

threat_actors.name: Lapsus$
threat_actors.description: Claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against MAPFRE ASSURANCE, stating the data was stolen for a 'private party' and would not be publicly leaked. Known for social engineering, bribing insiders, and exploiting weak MFA implementations.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for a private party, suggesting a data-theft-for-hire or private sale scenario.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Described as a likely initial access vector for the MAPFRE attack, as Lapsus$ is proficient at social engineering help desks and employees to gain access to credentials and VPNs.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
attack_patterns.description: Described as a likely technique used against MAPFRE, where Lapsus$ employs MFA fatigue or 'push bombing' attacks by repeatedly sending MFA approval requests to a user's device.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: Described as a likely post-compromise technique for the MAPFRE attack, where Lapsus$ searches internal systems like Confluence, SharePoint, and Jira for additional credentials and sensitive information.

identities.name: MAPFRE ASSURANCE
identities.description: A major insurance company in Spain targeted by the Lapsus$ group in a data breach incident announced on May 31, 2026.

indicators.name: Lapsus-style MFA Fatigue Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential MFA Fatigue Attempt
id: 4e8f1b2c-9a0d-4f3c-8e7b-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects MFA push notifications which, when correlated, can indicate an MFA fatigue or 'push bombing' attack. This is a known TTP of groups like Lapsus$. High-fidelity detection requires correlating multiple events for the same user in a short time window.
references:
  - https://dexpose.io/lapsus-targets-mapfre-assurance-in-spain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1621
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user receiving multiple MFA prompts due to login issues.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    Status.AdditionalDetails|contains: 'MFA authentication notification has been sent'
    AuthenticationRequirement: 'multiFactorAuthentication'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects individual MFA push notification events. This activity is associated with the Lapsus$ group's TTP of MFA fatigue ('push bombing') and should be correlated to alert on a high volume of requests for a single user in a short time.
